name: Stuffed Squid with Chocolate Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fish, shellfish or chicken stock
1 oz Lbarra brand chocolate, coarsely grated
recipeInstructions:
Adapted from "Catalan Cuisine" by Colman Andrews.
1. Remove heads and tentacles from squid and set bodies aside.
2. Mince the heads and tentacles, then mix well with the ground pork (may use a food processor).
3. Heat about 1/2 inch of olive oil in a cassola or large skillet. Sauté the onion, carrot, garlic, and parsley until onions are wilted. Add the pork mixture, crumbs, and half the pine nuts, mixing together well and cooking until meat is well done. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
4. Remove mixture from pan and drain on paper towels or in a colander.
5. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
6. When pork mixture is cool, lightly stuff the reserved squid bodies with it (do not overstuff, as this may cause the squid to shrink and tear during cooking). Bake the squid in a single layer, uncovered, in a lightly oiled baking dish for about 20 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, deglaze the cassola with stock and wine, simmering until reduced by about half.
8. While liquid reduces, put almonds, remaining pine nuts, chocolate, and fried bread in a mortar and pound with a pestle until finely ground. Mix with a bit of the liquid to make a thick paste (it must be fine, without grainy texture). Add this mixture to the reduced liquid, stir well, return to boil, and season to taste.
9. Pour sauce over stuffed squid or spoon onto serving plates and place squid on top of sauce.
Note: Lbarra chocolate is sweetened with almonds and cinnamon and is available in many markets in the Bay Area.
Calories per serving: 535
Protein: 31 g
Carbohydrate: 16 g
Fat (saturated): 40 g
Cholesterol: 239 mg
Sodium: 96 mg
Fiber: 3 g
